paper_title: Sampling and Morphological Analyses of Suncus niger

methods.description: Process for capturing Suncus niger specimens and taking external and craniodental measurements for morphological analysis.

steps.description: Three adult males of Suncus niger were captured near Hulikal camp, Mookambika Wildlife Sanctuary, Udupi, Karnataka, India (13.725 N 75.010 E) through a pitfall method.
steps.searchable_summary: capture Suncus niger specimens using pitfall method

steps.description: The external measurements were taken in the field, including length of head and body (HBL), length of tail (TL), height of ear (EH), and length of hindfoot excluding claw (HFL), as per standard method.
steps.searchable_summary: measure external features of Suncus niger specimens

steps.description: The craniodental measurements were taken after skull extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: extract skulls and measure craniodental features

methods.description: Comparison of morphological measurements of Suncus niger with other species using museum specimens and database records.

steps.description: The morphological analyses of S. niger were performed as per standard protocol and the external and craniodental measurements were compared with other closely related species from the available museum specimens at the National Zoological Collections of the Zoological Survey of India.
steps.searchable_summary: compare Suncus niger measurements with museum specimens

steps.description: Morphological measurements of S. zeylanicus were obtained from the Global Biodiversity Information Facility (GBIF) database.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch S. zeylanicus measurements from GBIF
